# Grouped drag executor operations: moveNode + transferNode

## Context
ADR 0029 §2.4's executor half (`learn/agentos/decisions/0029-harness-docking-design.md`) — the Qt-ADS bar: dragging a title bar moves the whole tabbed GROUP. Wholly unfiled per the ADR's §5 leaf table. Filed to the delegatable bar: **non-fable implementable**; the section is a complete op contract.

## The Problem
The dock tree already models the group — a `tabs` node — but the executor vocabulary only moves ITEMS. Grouped drag needs node-level operations: re-parent a subtree within one document, and transfer a subtree across documents, both preserving every member item and normalizing afterward.

## The Architectural Reality
- Executor: `src/dashboard/DockZoneModel.mjs`, same pure static family + fail-closed result contract.
- `moveNode` — `{operation: 'moveNode', nodeId, targetNodeId, placement}` within one document: re-parents the subtree per the landed placement descriptors; `normalizeTree` guarantees invariants after (ADR §2.4 verbatim).
- `transferNode` — `#14768`'s `transferItem` semantics applied to a subtree: atomic two-document commit, ALL member item records travel verbatim, hints update per item. Reuses `#14768`'s machinery — no second atomicity implementation.
- Preview layer: ONE additive runtime-only field `groupNodeId` on the existing `dockPreview` payload (set when the drag source is a group handle); `dockPreview` stays v1; the field never persists. Placement kinds unchanged; a group dropped `tab-into` merges its items into the target tabs node in order.
- Cycle guard: moving a node into its own subtree must fail closed (the landed fingerprint-walk cycle discipline extends to placement validation).

## The Fix
One PR: both ops + the `groupNodeId` preview-field documentation + specs (moveNode re-parent + normalize; into-own-subtree fail-closed; transferNode atomicity via forced target failure; member records verbatim; tab-into merge order; unknown node/target fail-closed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `moveNode` re-parents per placement descriptor; invariants hold post-normalize (spec).
- [ ] Into-own-subtree and unknown-reference cases fail closed, original document untouched (spec).
- [ ] `transferNode` reuses the `#14768` atomic path (commit-both-or-neither spec) with per-item hint updates.
- [ ] `groupNodeId` documented as runtime-only; persistence paths reject it (the landed preview-key rejection extends).
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
The group-handle drag surface / tab-bar drag source UI (rides the §2.7/#13280 line) · tab overflow (sibling leaf) · `#14768` itself.

## Related
Parent `#13158` · authority ADR 0029 §2.4 · blocked_by `#14768` (transferNode reuse) · joins `#14715`'s vocabulary. Decision Record impact: aligned-with ADR 0029.

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-07-05T05:55:58Z

## Claiming — blocker cleared + Contract Ledger (satisfies the 2026-07-04 intake gate)

@neo-gpt's intake verdict held this `needs-design`/`not-code-ready` on two conditions. Both are now met:

1. **Prerequisite #14768 (`transferItem`) LANDED** — merged at #14832 (`f41396ce4`). `transferNode` now reuses a real atomic two-document substrate instead of guessing an unlanded contract.
2. **Contract Ledger** (below) — the multi-surface matrix the Completeness Gate requires.

Lifting `not-code-ready` + `needs-design`; self-assigning. Re-intake welcome at PR review (condition 3).

### Contract Ledger

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `DockZoneModel.moveNode(document, {nodeId, targetNodeId, placement})` | ADR 0029 §2.4 | Re-parents the subtree rooted at `nodeId` to `targetNodeId` via the landed placement grammar; `normalizeTree` restores invariants after | Into-own-subtree → fail-closed (cycle guard via the reachable-set walk); unknown node/target → fail-closed, document untouched | JSDoc + this ledger | unit: re-parent + normalize; into-own-subtree rejected; unknown-ref rejected |
| `DockZoneModel.transferNode(sourceDocument, targetDocument, {nodeId, …, target})` | ADR 0029 §2.4 + landed §2.3 `transferItem` | `transferItem` atomicity applied to a subtree: every member item record travels verbatim, the subtree re-homes, commit-both-or-neither — reuses the landed atomic path, no second atomicity impl | Any member/placement failure → both documents byte-untouched + errors; `movable:false` member / id-collision / same-workspace reject | JSDoc + ledger | unit: atomic rollback on forced target failure; all member records verbatim; commit-both-or-neither |
| `dockPreview.groupNodeId` — runtime-only preview field | ADR 0029 §2.4 | Optional additive marker of a group-handle drag source; `dockPreview` stays v1; never persisted | Joins `forbiddenPreviewKeys` — every persistence/projection path rejects a committed doc carrying it | ledger + inline | unit: a committed document carrying `groupNodeId` is rejected |
| `tab-into` group merge order | ADR 0029 §2.4 | A group dropped `tab-into` merges its member items into the target tabs node in committed order | — | ledger | unit: merge preserves item order |

### Scope confirm (per the ticket)
IN: both executor ops + the `groupNodeId` runtime-only rejection + cycle-guard + specs. OUT (unchanged): the group-handle/tab-bar drag **surface** UI (rides the §2.7 line), tab overflow (sibling leaf), live-instance reparenting + durable hint updates (the #14769 wiring leaf — the same boundary `transferItem` held).

- 2026-07-05T10:42:39Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `20316ce` - "fix(dashboard): reject runtime-only preview keys on the persistence path, not just projection (#14770)

Euclid's review found the Contract Ledger's "groupNodeId never persisted" claim
was only half-implemented: the adapter rejected it at projection, but
DockZoneModel.createSavedLayout() / restoreSavedLayout() let it ride through the
opaque items.*.metadata channel into a durable saved layout.

Fix at the model boundary (the serialization SSOT): forbiddenPreviewKeys +
findForbiddenPreviewKey move from DockLayoutAdapter into DockZoneModel, and
validate() rejects any document carrying a forbidden preview key anywhere,
including nested metadata. createSavedLayout, restoreSavedLayout, and the commit
path all validate through this choke point, so persistence-rejection and
projection-rejection now share one set and cannot drift. The adapter imports the
model's set at the projection boundary (single SSOT, duplicate removed).

5 new specs: validate / createSavedLayout / restoreSavedLayout reject a smuggled
groupNodeId (the review falsifier), a clean doc round-trips unaffected, and the
SSOT set membership. 137 dashboard + consumer specs green."
- 2026-07-05T11:08:31Z @tobiu referenced in commit `7a5dcd2` - "feat(dashboard): grouped-drag executor — moveNode + transferNode subtree ops (#14770) (#14835)

* feat(dashboard): grouped-drag executor — moveNode + transferNode subtree ops (#14770)

The node-level (subtree) half of ADR-0029 §2.4 grouped drag: the dock tree
already models a group as a tabs node, so grouped drag moves a NODE, not N items.

- moveNode(document, {nodeId, targetNodeId, placement}): re-parents a subtree
  within one document via the landed placement grammar (split wrap or tab-into
  merge); normalizeTree restores invariants. Fail-closed on unknown refs, the
  root, self-move, invalid placement, and moving a node into its own subtree
  (cycle guard via the reachable-set walk).
- transferNode(source, target, {nodeId, ..., target}): transferItem atomicity
  applied to a whole subtree — every member node + item record travels verbatim,
  commit-both-or-neither, reusing the landed atomic path. Fail-closed on node-id
  / member-id collision, unmovable member, root, same-workspace.
- Shared detachNode/attachNode helpers (attachNode reused by both ops; detachNode
  renormalizes surviving split sizes to an exact sum of 1).
- groupNodeId joins DockLayoutAdapter.forbiddenPreviewKeys (runtime-only preview
  field, never persisted).
- Both ops join the derived operations SSOT: moveNode as a real single-document
  handler, transferNode as a two-document redirect.

The DockService consumer's unknown-operation test now derives its enumerated
vocabulary from the SSOT rather than a hardcoded literal, so it stays green as
the operation family grows.

15 new specs (7 moveNode, 7 transferNode, 1 groupNodeId); 132 dashboard + consumer specs green.

* fix(dashboard): reject runtime-only preview keys on the persistence path, not just projection (#14770)

Euclid's review found the Contract Ledger's "groupNodeId never persisted" claim
was only half-implemented: the adapter rejected it at projection, but
DockZoneModel.createSavedLayout() / restoreSavedLayout() let it ride through the
opaque items.*.metadata channel into a durable saved layout.

Fix at the model boundary (the serialization SSOT): forbiddenPreviewKeys +
findForbiddenPreviewKey move from DockLayoutAdapter into DockZoneModel, and
validate() rejects any document carrying a forbidden preview key anywhere,
including nested metadata. createSavedLayout, restoreSavedLayout, and the commit
path all validate through this choke point, so persistence-rejection and
projection-rejection now share one set and cannot drift. The adapter imports the
model's set at the projection boundary (single SSOT, duplicate removed).

5 new specs: validate / createSavedLayout / restoreSavedLayout reject a smuggled
groupNodeId (the review falsifier), a clean doc round-trips unaffected, and the
SSOT set membership. 137 dashboard + consumer specs green."
